data.title: Intelligent Bio Solutions addresses cybersecurity issues, keeps 510(k) submission on track for H2 2026
data.description:
Intelligent Bio Solutions resolved all cybersecurity and software review items ahead of a planned FDA 510(k) submission in H2 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Completed independent penetration testing and remediated all cybersecurity review items, reducing regulatory risk.
  • 100% of medium-risk vulnerabilities fixed; low-risk issues cut from 11 to 2 with risk-based justifications provided.
  • All 19 SBOM vulnerabilities resolved and documentation aligned with FDA guidance; third-party verification found no remaining issues.
  • 510(k) package remains on track for calendar H2 2026, targeting codeine detection to enable U.S. market entry beyond forensic use.
  • Manufacturing partnership expected to cut production costs >40% and improve gross margin by ~20 percentage points, supporting scalable launch.

Original SEC Filing: INTELLIGENT BIO SOLUTIONS INC. [ INBS ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: InspireMD Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On July 17, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that it failed to maintain the minimum $1.00 bid price under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) after 30 consecutive business days below the threshold. InspireMD has 180 days, until January 13, 2027, to regain compliance, and its stock continues to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol NSPR.

Original SEC Filing: InspireMD, Inc. [ NSPR ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

data.title: Auddia Extends $1.4 Million 8% Senior Bridge Notes to Thramann Portfolio Ahead of Merger
data.description:
Auddia entered into four senior unsecured bridge notes totaling up to $1.4 million with Thramann Holdings and its portfolio companies LT350, Influence Healthcare, and Voyex to provide interim funding while a pending merger is finalized. The notes carry 8% annual interest, are unsecured and senior, and mature upon the earlier of a change of control or two years after any termination of the merger agreement. Notes for LT350, Influence Healthcare, and Voyex include automatic conversion at 80% of price upon Qualified Financings meeting specified thresholds; Thramann Holdings’ note is not convertible. All funded amounts will count toward Auddia’s $12 million net cash closing condition under the merger agreement.

Agreement 1: Auddia Extends $360,000 8% Senior Bridge Note to Thramann Holdings Ahead of Merger

  • Agreement type: Senior unsecured bridge note at 8% interest
  • Counterparty: Thramann Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Earlier of change of control or two years after merger termination
  • Reason: Provide interim funding while merger is pending

Agreement 2: Auddia Provides $400,000 8% Convertible Bridge Note to LT350 Amid Pending Merger

  • Agreement type: Senior unsecured convertible bridge note at 8% interest
  • Counterparty: LT350
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Earlier of change of control or two years after merger termination
  • Reason: Provide interim funding while merger is pending

Agreement 3: Auddia Issues $590,000 8% Convertible Bridge Note to Influence Healthcare

  • Agreement type: Senior unsecured convertible bridge note at 8% interest
  • Counterparty: Influence Healthcare
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Earlier of change of control or two years after merger termination
  • Reason: Provide interim funding while merger is pending

Agreement 4: Auddia Executes $50,000 8% Convertible Bridge Note With Voyex

  • Agreement type: Senior unsecured convertible bridge note at 8% interest
  • Counterparty: Voyex
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Earlier of change of control or two years after merger termination
  • Reason: Provide interim funding while merger is pending

Original SEC Filing: AUDDIA INC. [ AUUD ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

HeartSciences Inc. FY 2026: Revenue $4.3K, EPS $(3.48) — 10-K Summary
data.description:
HeartSciences Inc. reported fiscal 2026 revenue of $4.3K, essentially flat with fiscal 2025, and a net loss that widened modestly to $9.142M from $8.765M a year earlier; diluted loss per share was $(3.48) for FY 2026 versus $(9.34) in FY 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $4.3K for FY 2026, flat vs $4.35K in FY 2025 (0% YoY).
  • Net income: Net loss of $9.142M for FY 2026 vs net loss of $8.765M in FY 2025 (loss widened by $377K, ≈4%).
  • Diluted EPS: Loss per share, basic and diluted, $(3.48) for FY 2026 vs $(9.34) for FY 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Product launch and upgrades: Released MyoVista Insights™ in May 2025 and rolled out a major v1.3 upgrade from March–June 2026 adding mobile access and an AI marketplace.
  • Regulatory pathway: Submitted MyoVista wavECG™ for FDA 510(k) clearance in December 2025; the company separated its AI e' algorithm for additional development following updated clinical guidelines.
  • Commercial traction: Secured first mainstream SaaS commercial deployments in May 2026 and onboarded an FDA-cleared third‑party AI through its marketplace in June 2026.
  • Platform interoperability and recognition: Earned Epic Toolbox designation in March 2026 to enhance Epic integration prospects.
  • R&D and accounting: R&D spending was reduced as phase 1 of MyoVista Insights™ completed; approximately $0.7M of software costs were capitalized to support SaaS commercialization.

Original SEC Filing: HeartSciences Inc. [ HSCS ] - 10-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: RingCentral reports Q2 2026 revenue $657M, GAAP EPS $0.45, raises dividend and outlook
data.description:
RingCentral posted second-quarter 2026 results with total revenue of $657.0 million, up 5.9% year-over-year, and GAAP diluted EPS of $0.45. The company reported non-GAAP diluted EPS of $1.22, non-GAAP operating margin of 23.4%, and free cash flow of $180 million. RingCentral's board increased the quarterly dividend to $0.125 per share and raised its full-year revenue, margin and free cash flow guidance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $657.0 million for Q2 2026, up 5.9% year-over-year (Q2 2025: $620.4 million).
  • Subscriptions revenue: $633.6 million for Q2 2026, up 5.8% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $472.3 million for Q2 2026 (as reported in the statement of operations).
  • Operating income (GAAP): $50.3 million for Q2 2026; non-GAAP income from operations: $154.0 million (23.4% non-GAAP operating margin).
  • Net income (GAAP): $39.1 million; GAAP diluted EPS: $0.45. Non-GAAP net income per diluted share: $1.22 for Q2 2026.
  • Cash flow and liquidity: Net cash provided by operating activities of $206 million for the quarter and free cash flow of $180 million; cash and cash equivalents at quarter-end $111.5 million.

Business Highlights

  • AI product adoption: Customers using at least one paid native AI product represent ~13% of ARR, doubling year-over-year, reflecting increased traction of RingCentral's AI portfolio.
  • Product expansions: Expanded AIR Pro with agentic AI capabilities in RingCX, including native AI agents, autonomous AI-powered outbound outreach, and intelligent handoffs to live agents.
  • AVA enhancements: Introduced AI-powered Workflow Builder and conversational analytics for AVA, enabling natural-language workflow creation and instant operational insights.
  • Workforce and quality tools: Advanced RingWEM with Live Screen Monitoring to give supervisors real-time visibility and live coaching capability to improve quality management and workforce performance.
  • Industry recognition: Named to TIME’s America’s Best Companies 2026 and recognized as a leader in multiple analyst reports (Nucleus Research, Aragon Research, ISG, Metrigy) for AI, contact center and UCaaS capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: RingCentral, Inc. [ RNG ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: AmeriServ Financial posts July 2026 investor presentation highlighting asset growth, deposit strength, and capital priorities
data.description:
AmeriServ Financial made available an investor presentation highlighting a $1.46B community bank balance sheet, stable deposits and capital return plans.

Key Highlights:

  • $1.46B total assets, $1.01B total loans and $1.26B total deposits as of June 30, 2026, underscoring scale.
  • Wealth management division manages $2.8B customer assets and materially contributes to non‑interest income.
  • Non‑performing loans $7.8M (0.76% of loans) and ACL coverage 167% of NPAs as of June 30, 2026, showing improved asset quality.
  • Net interest margin improved to 3.15% in 2025 and recent quarterly NII and NIM trends point to continued benefits from asset repricing.
  • Board targets dividends (current quarterly rate $0.03), no buybacks authorized now; CBLR under review for capital metric use.

Original SEC Filing: AMERISERV FINANCIAL INC /PA/ [ ASRV ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026
